name: Berlin (Land Berlin / Bundeshauptstadt)
summary: Berlin (German: Land Berlin / Bundeshauptstadt 'Federal Capital') is a Northeastern German civil-law constituent Land of the Federal Republic of Germany + German federal capital — structurally distinctive globally as the only post-WWII jurisdiction divided continuously for 44 years (1945-1989) under formal four-power Allied occupation framework (US + UK + France + Soviet Union — Yalta Conference 4-11 February 1945 + Potsdam Conference 17 July - 2 August 1945 + Berlin Declaration 5 June 1945 + Four Powers Status of Berlin 12 September 1944, four-power-occupation framework continuous 1945-1990 only ended by Treaty on the Final Settlement with Respect to Germany 12 September 1990), as the central jurisdiction of the most distinctive modern divided-city Cold War framework (Berlin Wall Berliner Mauer 13 August 1961 - 9 November 1989 — 155 km concrete wall + barbed-wire framework dividing West Berlin from East Berlin + East Germany, with ~140 confirmed deaths attempting Berlin Wall crossings), as one of three German Stadtstaaten (city-state Länder operating simultaneously as Land + municipality — Berlin + Hamburg + Bremen, distinct from rest-of-German-Länder territorial framework), and as the central jurisdiction of one of the most distinctive modern German federal-capital-city frameworks (Berlin became German federal capital via German federal decision of 20 June 1991 effective 1999 — Bonn-to-Berlin Capital Move framework, only modern federal capital established via formal post-Cold-War-reunification political decision). Berlin was historically the capital of Brandenburg-Prussia 1701-1871 + capital of German Empire 1871-1918 + capital of Weimar Republic 1918-1933 + capital of Nazi Germany 1933-1945 + post-WWII four-power occupation 1945-1990 (with West Berlin operating as de facto West German territory + East Berlin as East German capital 1949-1990), reunified 3 October 1990, and re-established as German federal capital 1999. Berlin comprises ~891 km² with a population of ~3.7 million. Family-law framework operates under the federal German civil-law framework (Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch BGB 1896 Family Law portion + German Family-Law Reform Act 2024) applied uniformly across German Länder. The Kammergericht (Berlin's Higher Regional Court) is the apex regional court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Federal Court of Justice of Germany. Berlin is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Germany is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory and party (acceded 27 September 1990 effective 1 December 1990) — Berlin is governed by German Hague-Convention-1980 framework.

statutory_framework.title: Constitution of Berlin 1995
statutory_framework.relevance: Berlin Constitution adopted 23 November 1995 — formal post-reunification constitutional framework for Land Berlin.

statutory_framework.citation: Treaty on the Final Settlement with Respect to Germany 1990 (Two Plus Four Treaty)
statutory_framework.title: Two Plus Four Treaty 1990
statutory_framework.relevance: Treaty of 12 September 1990 between East Germany + West Germany + US + UK + France + Soviet Union — formal termination of four-power Allied occupation framework + final settlement of Berlin sovereignty.

statutory_framework.title: Four Powers Status of Berlin
statutory_framework.relevance: London Protocol of 12 September 1944 + Berlin Declaration of 5 June 1945 establishing formal four-power Allied occupation framework — continuous 1945-1990.

statutory_framework.title: Federal Basic Law of Germany
statutory_framework.relevance: German Federal Basic Law adopted 8 May 1949 — applicable to West Berlin via Federal Basic Law Article 23 framework, fully applicable to reunified Berlin from 3 October 1990.

statutory_framework.title: Bonn-Berlin Act
statutory_framework.relevance: German Federal Bonn-Berlin Act of 26 April 1994 — formal Bonn-to-Berlin Capital Move framework, effective 1999.

statutory_framework.title: German Civil Code BGB
statutory_framework.relevance: German Federal Civil Code 1896 Family Law portion applicable uniformly across Berlin.

statutory_framework.title: Hague Convention 1980 — Germany
statutory_framework.relevance: German Hague Convention 1980 accession 27 September 1990 effective 1 December 1990 — applicable to Berlin.

apex_courts.name: Kammergericht (Berlin Higher Regional Court)
apex_courts.role: Apex regional appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Berlin.

apex_courts.name: Federal Court of Justice of Germany (Bundesgerichtshof BGH)
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Berlin.

professional_regulators.name: German Medical Association (Bundesärztekammer)
professional_regulators.role: German Federal professional regulatory framework applicable in Berlin.

anonymisation_convention: Berlin family-court decisions are anonymised per German court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Berlin as Brandenburg-Prussia capital
key_developments.description: Berlin established as capital of Brandenburg-Prussia 1701 — beginning of formal capital framework 1701-1871.

key_developments.title: Berlin as German Empire capital
key_developments.description: Berlin established as capital of German Empire 18 January 1871 — German Empire 1871-1918.

key_developments.title: Four-power Allied occupation begins
key_developments.description: Berlin Declaration 5 June 1945 — formal four-power Allied occupation framework begins, continuous 1945-1990.

key_developments.title: Berlin Blockade
key_developments.description: Berlin Blockade 24 June 1948 - 12 May 1949 — Soviet Union blockade of West Berlin land routes + Western Allied Berlin Airlift framework.

key_developments.title: Berlin Wall constructed
key_developments.description: Berlin Wall Berliner Mauer constructed 13 August 1961 — 155 km concrete wall + barbed-wire framework dividing West Berlin from East Berlin + East Germany.

key_developments.title: Berlin Wall falls
key_developments.description: Berlin Wall fall 9 November 1989 — end of 28-year Berlin Wall framework.

key_developments.title: German reunification + Berlin sovereignty restoration
key_developments.description: German reunification 3 October 1990 + Two Plus Four Treaty 12 September 1990 — formal termination of four-power Allied occupation framework + restoration of full Berlin sovereignty.

key_developments.title: Bonn-Berlin Capital Move decision
key_developments.description: German Bundestag decision of 20 June 1991 — Bonn-to-Berlin Capital Move framework decision.

key_developments.title: Constitution of Berlin
key_developments.description: Constitution of Berlin adopted 23 November 1995 — formal post-reunification constitutional framework for Land Berlin.

key_developments.title: Federal capital effective Berlin
key_developments.description: Bonn-to-Berlin Capital Move effective 1 September 1999 — formal federal capital framework restored.

structural_findings: Berlin operates a federal German civil-law constituent Land + city-state framework — places Berlin in the Northeastern German Stadtstaat cluster.

Only post-WWII jurisdiction divided continuously for 44 years (1945-1989) under formal four-power Allied occupation framework is structurally distinctive globally — US + UK + France + Soviet Union four-power-occupation framework continuous 1945-1990.

Central jurisdiction of most distinctive modern divided-city Cold War framework is structurally distinctive globally — Berlin Wall 13 August 1961 - 9 November 1989, 155 km wall + ~140 confirmed deaths.
One of three German Stadtstaaten (city-state Länder operating simultaneously as Land + municipality) is structurally distinctive within German Land cluster — Berlin + Hamburg + Bremen.
Central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern German federal-capital-city frameworks is structurally distinctive globally — Bonn-to-Berlin Capital Move 1991-1999 framework.

Brandenburg-Prussia capital 1701-1871 + German Empire 1871-1918 + Weimar Republic 1918-1933 + Nazi Germany 1933-1945 + reunified Germany 1990+ continuous capital framework is structurally distinctive globally.
1948-1949 Berlin Blockade + Berlin Airlift framework is structurally distinctive globally — pivotal Cold War armed-stand-off framework.
editorial_notes: Berlin jurisdiction sidecar — civil-law Northeastern German constituent Land + Stadtstaat + federal capital (Constitution of Berlin 1995 + Two Plus Four Treaty 1990 + Four Powers Status of Berlin 1944/1945 + Federal Basic Law of Germany 1949 + Bonn-Berlin Act 1994 + German Civil Code BGB 1896 + Brandenburg-Prussia capital 1701-1871 + German Empire capital 1871-1918 + Berlin Blockade 1948-1949 + Berlin Wall 1961-1989 + reunification 1990 + federal capital effective 1999 + German Hague-1980). Only post-WWII jurisdiction divided continuously for 44 years under formal four-power Allied occupation framework globally + central jurisdiction of most distinctive modern divided-city Cold War framework + one of three German Stadtstaaten + central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern German federal-capital-city frameworks + continuous capital-of-Germany framework 1871-present.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Northeastern-German + civil-law + German-Land + German-Stadtstaat cluster + four-power-Allied-occupation-1945-1990-globally-distinctive + Berlin-Wall-1961-1989-divided-city-Cold-War-framework + Two-Plus-Four-Treaty-1990 + Bonn-Berlin-Capital-Move-1991-1999 + Berlin-Blockade-1948-1949 + continuous-capital-of-Germany-1871-present + German-Hague-1980-1990 clusters within the corpus.
